001package com.jwoglom.pumpx2.pump.messages.response.currentStatus;
002
003import com.jwoglom.pumpx2.pump.messages.bluetooth.Characteristic;
004import com.jwoglom.pumpx2.pump.messages.Message;
005import com.jwoglom.pumpx2.pump.messages.MessageType;
006import com.jwoglom.pumpx2.pump.messages.annotations.MessageProps;
007import com.jwoglom.pumpx2.pump.messages.helpers.Bytes;
008import com.jwoglom.pumpx2.pump.messages.request.currentStatus.CurrentActiveIdpValuesRequest;
009
010import org.apache.commons.lang3.Validate;
011
012/**
013 * Returns the currently active insulin delivery profile (IDP) parameter values
014 * in effect right now, based on the active time segment.
015 *
016 * Byte layout of the cargo (10 bytes total):
017 *   bytes 0-3:  currentCarbRatio (uint32 LE) - carb ratio in 1000-increments
018 *   byte  4:    unknown/padding
019 *   bytes 5-6:  currentTargetBg (uint16 LE) - target blood glucose in mg/dL
020 *   bytes 6-7:  currentInsulinDuration (uint16 LE) - insulin duration in minutes
021 *   bytes 8-9:  currentIsf (uint16 LE) - insulin sensitivity factor in mg/dL per unit
022 *
023 * Note: bytes 5-6 (currentTargetBg) and bytes 6-7 (currentInsulinDuration) share byte 6.
024 * In practice, currentTargetBg is always less than 256 (its high byte at position 6 is 0),
025 * so byte 6 carries only the low byte of currentInsulinDuration. This matches the decompiled
026 * Tandem Mobi Android app (CurrentActiveIDPValuesResponse$CurrentActiveIDPValuesCargo):
027 *   field c (C8235b, index 29) → currentCarbRatio  (readUint32 from offset 0)
028 *   field d (C8708a, index 0)  → currentTargetBg   (readUint16 from copyFrom(bytes, 5, 2))
029 *   field e (C8708a, index 1)  → currentInsulinDuration (readUint16 from copyFrom(bytes, 6, 2))
030 *   field f (C8708a, index 2)  → currentIsf         (readUint16 from copyFrom(bytes, 8, 2))
031 *
032 * @see com.jwoglom.pumpx2.pump.messages.response.currentStatus.IDPSegmentResponse
033 * @see com.jwoglom.pumpx2.pump.messages.response.currentStatus.IDPSettingsResponse
034 */
035/**
036 * Returns the currently active insulin delivery profile (IDP) parameter values
037 * in effect right now, based on the active time segment.
038 *
039 * Byte layout of the cargo (10 bytes total):
040 *   bytes 0-3:  currentCarbRatio (uint32 LE) - carb ratio in 1000-increments
041 *   byte  4:    unknown/padding
042 *   bytes 5-6:  currentTargetBg (uint16 LE) - target blood glucose in mg/dL
043 *   bytes 6-7:  currentInsulinDuration (uint16 LE) - insulin duration in minutes
044 *   bytes 8-9:  currentIsf (uint16 LE) - insulin sensitivity factor in mg/dL per unit
045 *
046 * Note: bytes 5-6 (currentTargetBg) and bytes 6-7 (currentInsulinDuration) share byte 6.
047 * In practice, currentTargetBg is always less than 256 (its high byte at position 6 is 0),
048 * so byte 6 carries only the low byte of currentInsulinDuration. This matches the decompiled
049 * Tandem Mobi Android app (CurrentActiveIDPValuesResponse$CurrentActiveIDPValuesCargo):
050 *   field c (C8235b, index 29) → currentCarbRatio  (readUint32 from offset 0)
051 *   field d (C8708a, index 0)  → currentTargetBg   (readUint16 from copyFrom(bytes, 5, 2))
052 *   field e (C8708a, index 1)  → currentInsulinDuration (readUint16 from copyFrom(bytes, 6, 2))
053 *   field f (C8708a, index 2)  → currentIsf         (readUint16 from copyFrom(bytes, 8, 2))
054 *
055 * @see com.jwoglom.pumpx2.pump.messages.response.currentStatus.IDPSegmentResponse
056 * @see com.jwoglom.pumpx2.pump.messages.response.currentStatus.IDPSettingsResponse
057 *
058
059 *
060 * <br>
061 *
062 * Response message for {@link com.jwoglom.pumpx2.pump.messages.request.currentStatus.CurrentActiveIdpValuesRequest}, opCode -105, size 10
063 *
064 * <br>
065 *
066 * 
067 * <table border='1'>
068 * <caption>Message properties:</caption>
069 * <tr><th>&nbsp;<code>opCode</code>&nbsp;</th><td>&nbsp;<code>-105</code>&nbsp;</td></tr>
070 * <tr><th>&nbsp;<code>size</code>&nbsp;</th><td>&nbsp;<code>10</code>&nbsp;</td></tr>
071 * <tr><th>&nbsp;<code>type</code>&nbsp;</th><td>&nbsp;<code>{@link com.jwoglom.pumpx2.pump.messages.MessageType#RESPONSE}</code>&nbsp;</td></tr>
072 * <tr><th>&nbsp;<code>characteristic</code>&nbsp;</th><td>&nbsp;<code>{@link com.jwoglom.pumpx2.pump.messages.bluetooth.Characteristic#CURRENT_STATUS}</code>&nbsp;</td></tr>
073 * <tr><th>&nbsp;<code>request</code>&nbsp;</th><td>&nbsp;<code>{@link com.jwoglom.pumpx2.pump.messages.request.currentStatus.CurrentActiveIdpValuesRequest}</code>&nbsp;</td></tr>
074 * </table>
075 */@MessageProps(
076    opCode=-105,
077    size=10,
078    type=MessageType.RESPONSE,
079    characteristic=Characteristic.CURRENT_STATUS,
080    request=CurrentActiveIdpValuesRequest.class
081)
082public class CurrentActiveIdpValuesResponse extends Message {
083
084    /**
085     * Carb ratio expressed in 1000-increments.
086     * A value of 10000 means 10 g/U.
087     * See {@link com.jwoglom.pumpx2.pump.messages.models.InsulinUnit#from1000To1(Long)}.
088     */
089    private long currentCarbRatio;
090
091    /**
092     * Target blood glucose in mg/dL used for Control-IQ corrections.
093     * Typical range: 80-200 mg/dL (always less than 256, so fits in a single byte).
094     */
095    private int currentTargetBg;
096
097    /**
098     * Duration of insulin action in minutes (Control-IQ insulin duration setting).
099     * Typical values: 120, 180, 240, or 300 minutes (2-5 hours).
100     */
101    private int currentInsulinDuration;
102
103    /**
104     * Insulin sensitivity factor (correction factor) in mg/dL per unit.
105     * A value of 30 means 1 U corrects 30 mg/dL.
106     */
107    private int currentIsf;
108
109    public CurrentActiveIdpValuesResponse() {}
110
111    public CurrentActiveIdpValuesResponse(long currentCarbRatio, int currentTargetBg, int currentInsulinDuration, int currentIsf) {
112        this.cargo = buildCargo(currentCarbRatio, currentTargetBg, currentInsulinDuration, currentIsf);
113        this.currentCarbRatio = currentCarbRatio;
114        this.currentTargetBg = currentTargetBg;
115        this.currentInsulinDuration = currentInsulinDuration;
116        this.currentIsf = currentIsf;
117    }
118
119    /**
120     * <table border="1">
121     * <caption>Parsed fields for CurrentActiveIdpValuesResponse:</caption>
122     * <tr><td><code>currentCarbRatio</code></td><td><code>Bytes.readUint32(raw, 0)</code></td></tr>
123     * <tr><td><code>currentTargetBg</code></td><td><code>Bytes.readShort(raw, 5)</code></td></tr>
124     * <tr><td><code>currentInsulinDuration</code></td><td><code>Bytes.readShort(raw, 6)</code></td></tr>
125     * <tr><td><code>currentIsf</code></td><td><code>Bytes.readShort(raw, 8)</code></td></tr>
126     * </table>
127     */
128    public void parse(byte[] raw) {
129        Validate.isTrue(raw.length == props().size());
130        this.cargo = raw;
131        // bytes 0-3: currentCarbRatio (uint32 LE)
132        this.currentCarbRatio = Bytes.readUint32(raw, 0);
133        // byte 4: unknown/padding — not parsed
134        // bytes 5-6: currentTargetBg (uint16 LE)
135        // Note: byte 6 is shared with currentInsulinDuration's low byte.
136        // In practice currentTargetBg < 256, so its high byte (byte 6) = 0.
137        this.currentTargetBg = Bytes.readShort(raw, 5);
138        // bytes 6-7: currentInsulinDuration in minutes (uint16 LE)
139        this.currentInsulinDuration = Bytes.readShort(raw, 6);
140        // bytes 8-9: currentIsf (uint16 LE)
141        this.currentIsf = Bytes.readShort(raw, 8);
142    }
143
144    /**
145     * Builds the cargo byte array for this response.
146     *
147     * Because {@code currentTargetBg} (bytes 5-6) and {@code currentInsulinDuration} (bytes 6-7)
148     * share byte 6, this method assumes {@code currentTargetBg < 256} (its high byte = 0) so
149     * that byte 6 carries only the low byte of {@code currentInsulinDuration} without conflict.
150     * <br>
151     * <table border="1">
152     * <caption>Byte layout for CurrentActiveIdpValuesResponse.buildCargo(...):</caption>
153     * <tr><th>Offset</th><th>Description</th></tr>
154     * <tr><td>0</td><td><code>toUint32(currentCarbRatio) [byte 0]</code></td></tr>
155     * <tr><td>1</td><td><code>toUint32(currentCarbRatio) [byte 1]</code></td></tr>
156     * <tr><td>2</td><td><code>toUint32(currentCarbRatio) [byte 2]</code></td></tr>
157     * <tr><td>3</td><td><code>toUint32(currentCarbRatio) [byte 3]</code></td></tr>
158     * <tr><td>4</td><td><code>(byte) (0)</code></td></tr>
159     * <tr><td>5</td><td><code>new byte[]{byte5} [element 0]</code></td></tr>
160     * <tr><td>6</td><td><code>new byte[]{byte6} [element 1]</code></td></tr>
161     * <tr><td>7</td><td><code>new byte[]{byte7} [element 2]</code></td></tr>
162     * <tr><td>8</td><td><code>firstTwoBytesLittleEndian(currentIsf) [byte 0]</code></td></tr>
163     * <tr><td>9</td><td><code>firstTwoBytesLittleEndian(currentIsf) [byte 1]</code></td></tr>
164     * </table>
165     */
166    public static byte[] buildCargo(long currentCarbRatio, int currentTargetBg, int currentInsulinDuration, int currentIsf) {
167        // The 3-byte region [5,6,7] covers:
168        //   byte 5 = targetBg low byte
169        //   byte 6 = targetBg high byte (0, since targetBg < 256) = insulinDuration low byte
170        //   byte 7 = insulinDuration high byte
171        byte byte5 = (byte) (currentTargetBg & 0xFF);
172        byte byte6 = (byte) (currentInsulinDuration & 0xFF);  // targetBg high byte assumed 0
173        byte byte7 = (byte) ((currentInsulinDuration >> 8) & 0xFF);
174
175        return Bytes.combine(
176            Bytes.toUint32(currentCarbRatio),           // bytes 0-3
177            new byte[]{0},                              // byte  4: unknown/padding
178            new byte[]{byte5, byte6, byte7},            // bytes 5-7: targetBg + insulinDuration (overlapping)
179            Bytes.firstTwoBytesLittleEndian(currentIsf) // bytes 8-9: ISF
180        );
181    }
182
183    /**
184     * @return carb ratio in 1000-increments. A value of 10000 means 10 g/U.
185     * See {@link com.jwoglom.pumpx2.pump.messages.models.InsulinUnit#from1000To1(Long)}.
186     */
187    public long getCurrentCarbRatio() {
188        return currentCarbRatio;
189    }
190
191    /**
192     * @return target blood glucose in mg/dL used for Control-IQ corrections
193     */
194    public int getCurrentTargetBg() {
195        return currentTargetBg;
196    }
197
198    /**
199     * @return Control-IQ insulin duration in minutes
200     */
201    public int getCurrentInsulinDuration() {
202        return currentInsulinDuration;
203    }
204
205    /**
206     * @return insulin sensitivity factor in mg/dL per unit
207     */
208    public int getCurrentIsf() {
209        return currentIsf;
210    }
211}
